channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Ruto Hosts First-Ever Madaraka Day Celebrations in Wajir
item.description: President William Ruto presided over the 2026 Madaraka Day celebrations at the newly built Wajir Stadium, marking a historic first for the North Eastern region of Kenya. The event, held on 1st June 20...

item.title: US and Iran exchange limited strikes amid fragile ceasefire and ongoing negotiations
item.description: Despite an existing ceasefire, the United States and Iran carried out fresh military strikes over the weekend, indicating continued tensions in West Asia. The US Central Command stated that it conduct...

item.title: Africa Accelerates Shift Toward Solar, Wind, and Distributed Energy Systems
item.description: Africa is rapidly expanding its renewable energy capacity as governments and investors prioritize solar, wind, and battery storage over traditional energy sources such as coal and large hydropower. Th...

item.title: WFP Expands Efforts to Address Food Security and Nutrition Gaps in Tanzania
item.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) has stepped up its commitment to support Tanzania in addressing ongoing food security and nutrition challenges affecting millions of people. Despite steady economic grow...

item.title: CBC to restructure over 700 government training institutions under UNNATI initiative
item.description: The Capacity Building Commission (CBC) is currently leading a major structural transformation of more than 700 training institutions across India that are responsible for training government employees...

item.title: New Virtual Museum Offers Access to Hundreds of Historic and Obsolete Operating Systems
item.description: A new digital preservation project known as the Virtual OS Museum allows users to explore and run around 570 historic and obsolete operating systems. The initiative has gained attention after reports ...

item.title: Jason Feger leads flag-to-flag to win $5,000 MARS Late Model feature at Red Hill Speedway
item.description: Jason Feger delivered a dominant performance on Saturday night, leading every lap to capture the MARS Late Model Championship Series feature at Red Hill Speedway in Sumner, Illinois. The 30-lap main e...

item.title: DA inspects Manila rice vendor over alleged alteration of P50 price cap signage
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) conducted an inspection in Manila to monitor compliance with the government’s P50 per kilo rice price cap initiative. During the inspection, authorities reportedly d...

item.title: Oyo school abduction triggers teachers’ strike as federal government deploys rescue team
item.description: Teachers in Oyo State under the Nigeria Union of Teachers (NUT) have begun an indefinite strike following the continued abduction of teachers and pupils from schools in Oriire Local Government Area. T...

item.title: New targeted daily pill shows significant survival improvement for advanced pancreatic cancer patients
item.description: A new clinical trial has shown that a once-daily pill called daraxonrasib can significantly extend the survival of patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, offering what experts have described as a m...

item.title: Sandiganbayan issues arrest warrant vs Jinggoy Estrada over plunder case
item.description: Former Philippine senator Jinggoy Estrada is facing a new arrest warrant issued in connection with a plunder case classified as non-bailable, according to a report from ABS-CBN News. The case is being...

item.title: Ethiopia holds nationwide election amid conflict as Ebola outbreak response and regional security concerns dominate Africa news
item.description: Across Africa, major developments have been reported on June 1, 2026, highlighting political, health, and security challenges across the continent. In Ethiopia, millions of voters are heading to the p...

item.title: Lesotho expands clean energy access, reaching 147,000 people in rural electrification drive
item.description: Lesotho has made important progress in improving access to electricity through its Clean Energy Access Transformation initiative, which has now benefited more than 147,000 people across the country. T...

item.title: Australian shares edge lower as oil prices rise and DroneShield faces shareholder backlash
item.description: Australian shares finished lower on Monday as investors monitored developments surrounding a potential long-term ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran. Ongoing uncertainty over the St...

item.title: Key outcomes and controversies from the first week of Senate estimates hearings
item.description: The first week of Australia’s Senate estimates hearings has revealed significant developments across the public service, including workforce reductions, funding pressures, accountability concerns, and...

item.title: Rural Eastern Cape still reflects apartheid spatial neglect, argues Lehohla
item.description: Pali Lehohla argues that the enduring poverty in Xolobe and Qutubeni, the rural birthplaces of Albertina and Walter Sisulu, reflects the unfinished business of South Africa’s democratic transition. Ra...

item.title: Karachi Traffic Police implement e-challan system for lane discipline on Sharea Faisal
item.description: Starting June 1, 2026, the Karachi Traffic Police have officially activated the e-challan system to monitor and penalise lane violations on Sharea Faisal under the Traffic Regulation and Citation Syst...

item.title: Full list of films and TV series leaving Netflix UK and US in June 2026
item.description: Netflix subscribers in the UK and US face a significant content purge in June 2026, with a wide range of films and television series scheduled to leave the platform throughout the month. The monthly r...

item.title: Los Angeles Officials Reaffirm Availability of In-Person Voting Ahead of Primary Election
item.description: In the days leading up to California’s June 2 primary election, former President Donald Trump made inaccurate claims suggesting that Los Angeles conducts elections exclusively by mail and lacks in-per...

item.title: Governance failures across Australian universities draw increasing scrutiny amid leadership controversies
item.description: Education commentator Julie Hare argues that governance problems are affecting a significant number of Australian universities, extending well beyond the highly publicised issues at the Australian Nat...
